## Structure

UDP C→S 0x03/0x07/0x01 — **Client-side CharInfo multipart**
(disc=0x01). 1821-byte reassembled body / 2 fragments.

```
[0..5]   multipart_header (per multipart_framing.md)
[6..]    [0x01 discriminator=CharInfo][total_size LE32][payload]
```

Sample inner (first 32B):
```
00 00 02 00 00 00 01 1d 07 00 00 21 1e 2c 00 00 00 b6 cc 01 00 49 6d 70 6f 72 74 61 6e 74 20 4e
                                        char_uid       "Important N..."
```

ASCII "Important N..." suggests this carries a **mail body** /
**important note** that the client is uploading to the server
— likely a saved-mail / draft submission. Despite the
discriminator-1 being labeled "CharInfo", the C→S direction
appears to carry user-generated content rather than character
state.
